title: melia azedarach subvar. semperflorens
intro: Melia azedarach subvar. semperflorens, also known as the perpetual flowering chinaberry or bead tree, is a subvariety of the Melia azedarach tree, native to Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antimalarial, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory compounds, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, skin conditions, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Melia azedarach subvar. semperflorens include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential natural remedy for various ailments. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
